summary.L2_brief: Hologic reported Q1 2026 revenue of $1.05 billion, up 2.5% year-over-year, but net income declined to $179.1 million ($0.79 EPS) from $201.0 million ($0.87 EPS) in the prior year. The company has a definitive merger agreement with Blackstone and TPG for $76.00 per share plus up to $3.00 CVR, expected to close in the first half of calendar 2026. A voluntary recall of Brevera 9 Gauge Needles was implemented following internal review of post-market data.

financial_performance.revenue: $1,047.8 million, up 2.5% from $1,021.8 million in Q1 2025
financial_performance.net_income: $179.1 million, down from $201.0 million in Q1 2025
financial_performance.eps: Diluted EPS $0.79, down from $0.87 in Q1 2025
financial_performance.free_cash_flow: Operating cash flow $229.9 million vs $189.3 million in Q1 2025
other_metrics: Effective tax rate 22.0% vs 18.8% prior year

segment_performance: Product revenues increased $13.5 million or 1.7% to $831.4 million
Service and other revenues increased $12.5 million or 6.1% to $216.4 million
L3_detailed.guidance_and_outlook: CVR payments contingent on achieving certain global revenue metrics for Breast Health business in fiscal 2026 and 2027. Brevera 9 Gauge Needles stop-ship may be prolonged as company re-engineers manufacturing process.

balance_sheet_highlights.total_debt: $2,508.7 million including $1,169 million term loan and $1,350 million senior notes

risk_factors: Timing and receipt of governmental and regulatory approvals for proposed merger
Supply chain constraints and cost inflation including $15.3 million tariff cost increase
Cybersecurity threats and targeted computer crime
Interest rate risk on variable rate debt

ma_and_deal_relevance.pending_deals: Definitive merger agreement with Blackstone and TPG for $76.00 per share plus up to $3.00 CVR, total up to $79.00 per share
ma_and_deal_relevance.deal_impact: Transaction expected to close in first half of calendar 2026 subject to stockholder approval and regulatory approvals. Purported stockholder lawsuits filed challenging merger proxy disclosures.
ma_and_deal_relevance.strategic_alternatives: Company expects to continue reviewing and evaluating potential strategic transactions
ma_and_deal_relevance.acquisition_activity: Gynesonics acquisition completed January 2, 2025 for $340.7 million purchase price. Strategic investment in Maverix Medical LLC with 45% ownership and $27.2 million net investment.

legal_and_regulatory: BioZorb litigation settlement reached with no financial liability to company, fully covered by insurance
Voluntary field safety notice and recall of Brevera 9 Gauge Needles issued January 2026
key_developments: Brevera 9 Gauge Needles stop-ship implemented mid-November 2025 following internal review
Manchester manufacturing facility ceased production in mid-November 2025
Discontinuing sale of Fluoroscan Insight FD systems effective in fiscal 2026
No stock option grants in Q1 2026 pursuant to merger agreement
L3_detailed.capital_allocation: New stock repurchase program authorized for up to $1.5 billion with $937.5 million remaining unused. Interest expense decreased to $27.6 million from $30.5 million due to debt refinancing.

summary.L1_headline: + HOLX – Diana De Walt disposed of 4,416 shares (tax withholding on RSU vesting). | 01/30/2026
summary.L2_brief: Diana De Walt, SVP of Human Resources at Hologic Inc, disposed of 4,416 shares of common stock on 01/30/2026 at $74.93 per share via tax withholding upon settlement of restricted stock units. This is an amended filing (Form 4/A) correcting a Power of Attorney attachment error from the original filing on 02/03/2026; the transaction details are unchanged. Post-transaction direct holdings stand at 7,603 shares.

L3_detailed.pattern_notes: Transaction code 'F' indicates shares were withheld by the issuer to cover tax obligations upon settlement of vested restricted stock units. This is a routine, non-discretionary disposition. The filing is a Form 4/A amendment filed solely to correct an attached Power of Attorney document; no transaction data was revised from the original 02/03/2026 filing.
risks_flagged: Disposition is non-discretionary (tax withholding on RSU vesting, transaction code F)
Shares disposed (4,416) represent approximately 37% of post-transaction holdings (7,603), though this is mechanical rather than voluntary selling

The grant represents approximately 6.3% of Stamoulis's post-transaction holdings of 50,526 shares. No open-market purchase or sale activity is indicated.
risks_flagged: No 10b5-1 plan indicated; this is a non-discretionary director compensation grant

Transaction code 'F' indicates shares withheld by the issuer to satisfy tax obligations, not a discretionary sale. The 185 shares disposed represent a small fraction (approximately 0.66%) of Liddy's total direct holdings of 28,052 shares.

summary.L1_headline: HOLX – Blackstone/TPG to acquire Hologic for $76+CVR. | 10/21/25
summary.L2_brief: Hologic entered into a definitive merger agreement with affiliates of Blackstone and TPG providing for acquisition at $76.00 per share in cash plus one CVR worth up to $3.00 based on Breast Health revenue milestones in fiscal years 2026 and 2027. The deal requires stockholder majority approval, HSR clearance, CFIUS review, and other regulatory approvals, with an outside date of July 21, 2026 (extendable by three months). A go-shop period runs until December 5, 2025, with a $540 million Company termination fee ($225 million if terminated for an Excluded Party) and a $900 million Parent termination fee; the deal is not subject to a financing condition.

L3_detailed.event: Hologic entered into an Agreement and Plan of Merger with Hopper Parent Inc. and Hopper Merger Sub Inc. (affiliates of Blackstone and TPG), under which Merger Sub will merge with and into Hologic, with Hologic surviving as a wholly owned subsidiary of Parent.
key_figures: CVR: up to $3.00 per share (up to $1.50 for FY2026 Breast Health revenue milestone, up to $1.50 for FY2027 milestone)
FY2026 CVR: $0.50-$1.50 linear interpolation for Breast Health revenue between $1,556,844,377 and $1,571,844,377; $1.50 if ≥$1,571,844,377
FY2027 CVR: $0.50-$1.50 linear interpolation for Breast Health revenue between $1,651,256,283 and $1,666,256,283; $1.50 if ≥$1,666,256,283
FY2027 catch-up mechanism if FY2026 CVR paid less than $1.50
Company termination fee: $540,000,000 (or $225,000,000 for Excluded Party prior to Cut-off Date)
Debt financing: $9,500M first lien term loans, $2,000M second lien term loans, $750M revolving credit facility
Company cash and short-term investments: ~$2.2 billion as of September 27, 2025
Company debt: ~$2.5 billion as of September 27, 2025
L3_detailed.deal_implications: Board unanimously approved and recommends the transaction. No financing condition; equity commitments from Blackstone/TPG affiliates plus debt commitments plus company cash are stated as sufficient to fund the purchase price. Go-shop period allows solicitation of competing bids through December 5, 2025, with Excluded Parties permitted to continue discussions through ten business days after go-shop expiration.
remaining_conditions: Adoption of merger agreement by holders of a majority of outstanding shares of Company Common Stock
HSR Act waiting period expiration or termination
Other required antitrust and foreign direct investment governmental approvals
No law or order prohibiting consummation of the Merger
Accuracy of representations and warranties (subject to materiality qualifiers)
Performance of covenants in all material respects
No continuing Material Adverse Effect on the Company (Parent/Merger Sub closing condition)
risks_flagged: CVRs are non-tradeable and non-transferable (except in limited circumstances); no assurance milestones will be achieved
CFIUS review required
Multiple antitrust/foreign direct investment approvals required across jurisdictions
Risk of litigation relating to the proposed transaction noted in forward-looking statements
Stockholder approval required; not yet obtained
Go-shop period may yield competing proposals

summary.L1_headline: HOLX – Q1 FY2026 earnings released amid pending Blackstone/TPG acquisition. | 01/29/26
summary.L2_brief: Hologic reported Q1 FY2026 financial results for the quarter ended December 27, 2025. The filing references the pending acquisition by affiliates of Blackstone Inc. and TPG Capital, for which a Definitive Proxy Statement was filed with the SEC on December 23, 2025 and mailed to stockholders. No changes to deal terms or timeline were disclosed in this filing.

L3_detailed.event: Hologic issued a press release announcing financial results for Q1 FY2026 (quarter ended December 27, 2025). The 8-K also includes standard disclosure language regarding the proposed acquisition by Blackstone and TPG affiliates.
key_figures: Definitive Proxy Statement filed with SEC on December 23, 2025
L3_detailed.deal_implications: The filing does not disclose any changes to the proposed Blackstone/TPG acquisition terms, timeline, or conditions. Hologic continues to operate and report earnings while the transaction is pending. The stockholder vote date was not specified in this filing.
remaining_conditions: Stockholder approval (Definitive Proxy Statement filed and mailed; vote date not specified in this filing)
Regulatory approvals (not detailed in this filing)
Other customary closing conditions (not detailed in this filing)
risks_flagged: Filing contains forward-looking statements with cautionary language noting actual results could differ materially from those expressed or implied

summary.filer_name: T. Rowe Price Investment Management, Inc.
summary.L1_headline: + HOLX – T. Rowe Price Investment Management discloses 9.7% stake. | 11/14/25
summary.L2_brief: T. Rowe Price Investment Management, Inc. filed Amendment No. 4 to its Schedule 13G disclosing beneficial ownership of 21,550,364 shares of Hologic Inc common stock, representing 9.7% of the class. The filing is made under Rule 13d-1(b)(1)(ii)(E) as a registered investment adviser, and the filer certifies the shares were acquired in the ordinary course of business and not for the purpose of changing or influencing control of the issuer.

L3_detailed.position_change: Amendment No. 4; prior position percentage not disclosed in this filing
L3_detailed.purpose_of_transaction: Shares acquired and held in the ordinary course of business. Per Item 10 certification: 'the securities referred to above were acquired and are held in the ordinary course of business and were not acquired and are not held for the purpose of or with the effect of changing or influencing the control of the issuer of the securities and were not acquired and are not held in connection with or as a participant in any transaction having that purpose or effect.'
L3_detailed.activist_intentions: None stated. Filed under Rule 13d-1(b) as a passive investment adviser. Filer expressly denies beneficial ownership is held for the purpose of influencing control.
L3_detailed.deal_implications: T. Rowe Price holds a 9.7% passive stake, making it a significant shareholder whose vote would be material in any future transaction requiring shareholder approval.
risks_flagged: Large passive holder whose position could be material in any shareholder vote on a potential transaction

summary.L1_headline: HOLX – Q4 earnings released amid pending Blackstone/TPG acquisition. | 11/03/25
summary.L2_brief: Hologic reported financial results for Q4 ended September 27, 2025. The filing includes soliciting material language related to the proposed acquisition of Hologic by affiliates of Blackstone Inc. and TPG Capital, noting that a definitive proxy statement will be filed with the SEC. No specific deal terms, vote dates, or financial figures from the earnings release were included in the 8-K text itself.

L3_detailed.event: Hologic issued a press release announcing Q4 FY2025 financial results. The 8-K was simultaneously filed as soliciting material under Rule 14a-12 in connection with the proposed acquisition by Blackstone and TPG Capital affiliates.
key_figures: No specific deal price, vote percentages, or financial figures disclosed in the 8-K body (earnings details are in Exhibit 99.1)
L3_detailed.deal_implications: The filing confirms the proposed Blackstone/TPG acquisition is still pending. Hologic stated it will file a proxy statement with the SEC and mail a definitive proxy to stockholders. No changes to deal status or timeline were disclosed in this filing.
remaining_conditions: Proxy statement to be filed with the SEC
Regulatory approvals (not specifically addressed in this filing)
risks_flagged: Forward-looking statements cautionary language referenced regarding risks that could cause actual results to differ materially
No litigation or specific regulatory issues mentioned in this filing
